tossups.question: In a painting titled for one of these events, numerous citizens - most prominently, a desperate woman in a yellow dress who faces away from the viewer - gesture towards a man giving his benediction from a loggia. At the left of that painting titled for one of these, a muscular nude dangles from a wall near another nude man carrying an old man on his back. Vasari notes that Sandro Botticelli became destitute after willingly giving over several paintings during this kind of event. A painting titled for one of these events names a (*) room whose frescoes depict The Coronation of Charlemagne, The Battle of Ostia, and The Oath of Leo III. That stanza decorated by Raphael is named for a painting of a miracle involving one of these events in which Leo IV saved the Borgo from destruction. For 10 points, identify these events, one of which was created by Savonarola and friends in 1497 to destroy art and lots of other things.
tossups.answer: fires [or bonfires; accept The Fire in the Borgo or Bonfire of the Vanities]
tossups.answer_sanitized: fires [or bonfires; accept The Fire in the Borgo or Bonfire of the Vanities]

Vasari notes that Sandro Botticelli became destitute after willingly giving over several paintings during this kind of event. A painting titled for one of these events names a (*) room whose frescoes depict The Coronation of Charlemagne, The Battle of Ostia, and The Oath of Leo III. That stanza decorated by Raphael is named for a painting of a miracle involving one of these events in which Leo IV saved the Borgo from destruction. For 10 points, identify these events, one of which was created by Savonarola and friends in 1497 to destroy art and lots of other things.

bonuses.leadin: In this painting, an old man wearing a Santa-hat-like cap is carried to safety in a scene inspired by Aeneas bearing Anchises away from Troy. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In this painting, an old man wearing a Santa-hat-like cap is carried to safety in a scene inspired by Aeneas bearing Anchises away from Troy. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this fresco depicting an episode from the Liber Pontificalis centering on Pope Leo IV, who is seen on a balcony in the background.
Sydney Freedberg attributed The Fire in the Borgo to this student of Raphael. The Fall of the Giants is among the Mannerist frescoes that this artist created for the Palazzo del Te in Mantua.
Giulio designed the Palazzo del Te for Federico II Gonzaga, who was also a patron of this artist of Sacred and Profane Love.
parts_sanitized: Name this fresco depicting an episode from the Liber Pontificalis centering on Pope Leo IV, who is seen on a balcony in the background.
Sydney Freedberg attributed The Fire in the Borgo to this student of Raphael. The Fall of the Giants is among the Mannerist frescoes that this artist created for the Palazzo del Te in Mantua.
Giulio designed the Palazzo del Te for Federico II Gonzaga, who was also a patron of this artist of Sacred and Profane Love.
answers_sanitized: The Fire in the Borgo [or L'Incendio di Borgo]
Giulio Romano [or Giulio Romano or Giulio Pippi]
Titian [or Tiziano Vecellio or Tiziano Vecellio; or Titianus]

bonuses.leadin: This painting, finally completed during the reign of Pope Clement VII, contains a man in a yellow sash grabbing a column with a sign reading "IAM TANDEM CHRISTUM LIBERE PRO FITERI LICET". For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this painting, completed by Giulio Romano and Gianfrancesco Penni. This painting is located in a room which also depicts its title figure's baptism and vision.
The Donation of Constantine and The Donation of Rome were among the paintings finished by the assistants of this Renaissance master, who also painted The Parnassus for the Stanza della Segnatura.
This painting, mostly the work of Romano, lends its name to another of the Raphael Rooms. A nude man carries another nude on his back away from the title conflagration on the left of this canvas.
answers_sanitized: The Donation of Constantine [or The Donation of Rome]
